data.title: Crypto News: Markets Slash Odds of December Fed Rate Cut Below 50% as Crypto Sell-Off Deepens
data.description: The probability of a December interest-rate cut has fallen sharply to 45.9%, down from nearly 67% earlier this month, adding pressure to an already fragile crypto market.Market expectations for a U.S. Federal Reserve rate cut in December have weakened significantly, with CME Group data showing the odds of a 25-basis-point (bps) cut dropping to 45.9%. Earlier this month, on Nov. 7, nearly 67% of investors were betting on a cut.The shift reflects deteriorating market sentiment, persistent macro uncertainty and the ongoing correction across crypto assets.Rate-cut expectations for 2025 also face downward revisionsIn September, major financial institutions—including Goldman Sachs and Citigroup—forecast at least two to three rate cuts in 2025. However, the recent tightening in market conditions is raising doubts about the pace and size of policy easing next year.Why declining rate-cut odds matter for Bitcoin and cryptoInterest rate decisions play a critical role in crypto price performance.Lower rates → more liquidity, stronger demand for risk assets.Higher-for-longer rates → tighter liquidity, weaker crypto prices.The drop in December rate-cut expectations has reinforced the risk-off tone across risk markets. Crypto traders now expect more short-term downside until the Federal Reserve commits to a clearer easing cycle.Fed Chair Jerome Powell: “A December rate cut is far from guaranteed”Federal Reserve Chair Jerome Powell warned in October that policymakers were divided on whether additional easing was appropriate:“A further reduction in the policy rate at the December meeting is not a foregone conclusion — far from it.”The Fed delivered a 25 bps rate cut in October, but the move failed to lift crypto markets, which continued their decline. Analysts at 21Shares said the cut was “fully priced in months in advance.”Ray Dalio warns Fed policy may be fueling a bubbleEconomist and Bridgewater founder Ray Dalio cautioned that the Federal Reserve is cutting rates despite:Record-high asset pricesLow unemploymentLow credit spreadsHe warned the Fed may be stimulating the economy into a bubble, a pattern he says is characteristic of economies heading toward “hyperinflation and currency collapse.”

data.title: Bitcoin News Today: Bitcoin ETFs Log $866M in Outflows in Second-Worst Day Ever — Analysts Split on Whether BTC Is Entering a Bear Market
data.description: U.S. spot Bitcoin ETFs recorded $866 million in outflows on Thursday — the second-worst day on record — as BTC slid to a six-month low and investor demand weakened following the end of the 43-day U.S. government shutdown.Investor appetite for crypto-linked funds continued to deteriorate on Thursday despite renewed government funding. According to Farside Investors, U.S. spot Bitcoin ETFs saw $866 million in net outflows, marking their worst session since February 25, 2025, when daily outflows hit $1.14 billion. The outflows also marked the second consecutive day of withdrawals, even as President Donald Trump signed a funding bill on Wednesday that keeps the government open until Jan. 30, 2026.ETF demand weakens as Bitcoin drops to six-month lowThe sharp outflows mirrored Bitcoin’s price action, with BTC falling to its lowest level in six months. Analysts warn the slowdown in ETF demand is particularly significant since ETFs and corporate accumulation — led by Michael Saylor’s strategy — were the main drivers of Bitcoin’s rally earlier in 2025.Still, some on-chain analysts argue the bull market structure remains intact.CryptoQuant CEO: Bull market remains valid unless BTC loses $94KCryptoQuant founder and CEO Ki Young Ju said the market should not assume a confirmed bear cycle unless Bitcoin drops below $94,000 — the average cost basis for investors who accumulated BTC over the last 6–12 months.“I do not think the bear cycle is confirmed unless we lose that level,” Ju said on X.“I would rather wait than jump to conclusions.”The $94K threshold is viewed as a crucial line of defense for bulls.Bitwise CEO: “We may have already been in a bear market for 6 months”Not all analysts agree. Bitwise CEO Hunter Horsley said the traditional four-year cycle no longer applies due to the introduction of Bitcoin ETFs and a new U.S. administration, which he argues have reshaped market structure.“There’s a good chance we’ve been in a bear market for almost six months now and are almost through it,” Horsley wrote.“The setup for crypto right now has never been stronger.”XRP ETF posts strongest launch of 2025, signaling fresh altcoin demandDespite the weakness in Bitcoin ETFs, demand for regulated altcoin ETFs is emerging.The Canary Capital XRP (XRPC) ETF, the first U.S. ETF backed by spot XRP, launched Thursday with $58 million in first-day volume, outperforming all other ETF launches in 2025 — both crypto and traditional — according to Bloomberg ETF analyst Eric Balchunas.“$XRPC posted the biggest Day One volume of any ETF launched this year,” Balchunas wrote, noting it narrowly beat the Bitwise Solana ETF (BSOL), which saw $57 million.The strong debut indicates continued investor interest in alternative crypto assets despite weakness at the top of the market.Ether ETFs bleed while Solana ETFs extend inflow streakETF flows across other major crypto assets remained mixed:Ether (ETH) ETFs saw $259 million in outflowsSolana (SOL) ETFs recorded $1.5 million in inflows, extending their 13-day streakBitcoin ETFs remained the primary source of outflows across the sector

data.title: Wall Street's Cautious Approach Amid Economic Data and Fed Rate Concerns
data.description:
According to PANews, Wall Street is exhibiting caution this week as the relief from the end of the historic U.S. government shutdown fades. Concerns are mounting over upcoming economic data and the Federal Reserve's ability to execute a rate cut in December. This risk-averse sentiment has intensified the sell-off of Bitcoin, which has fallen from its record high in early October, showing only slight gains in 2025. The leading digital asset has dropped below the $95,000 mark. CoinGecko data reveals that after a $19 billion liquidation on October 10, which led to a $1 trillion evaporation in the total cryptocurrency market value, the crypto market remains under pressure.

Key events this week include:

- Tuesday 02:00 (UTC+8): 2026 FOMC voting member and Minneapolis Fed President Kashkari hosts a fireside chat.

- Thursday 03:00 (UTC+8): The Federal Reserve releases its monetary policy meeting minutes, followed by a speech from FOMC permanent voting member and New York Fed President Williams.

- Friday 02:40 (UTC+8): 2025 FOMC voting member and Chicago Fed President Goolsbee delivers a speech.

- Friday 05:30 (UTC+8): 2026 FOMC voting member and Philadelphia Fed President Paulson discusses economic outlook.

- Friday 20:30 (UTC+8): FOMC permanent voting member and New York Fed President Williams speaks.

- Friday 22:00 (UTC+8): 2026 FOMC voting member and Dallas Fed President Logan participates in a panel at the "2025 Swiss National Bank and Its Observers" event.

The market has taken note of numerous comments from so-called "inflation hawks." Investors have reduced the likelihood of a December rate cut to below 50%. Prior to the Fed's October meeting, the market had almost fully priced in a December rate cut.
